# PROSPECTUS

# AURORA QUANTUM COMPUTING, INC.

## 28,000,000 Shares of Common Stock

### Initial Public Offering Price: $32.00 Per Share

---

This is the initial public offering of shares of common stock of Aurora Quantum Computing, Inc. ("Aurora," the "Company," "we," "us," or "our"). We are offering 22,000,000 shares and the selling stockholders identified in this prospectus are offering an additional 6,000,000 shares. We will not receive any proceeds from the sale of shares by the selling stockholders.

Prior to this offering, there has been no public market for our common stock. Our common stock has been approved for listing on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol "AURQ."

| | Per Share | Total |
|---|---|---|
| Initial public offering price | $32.00 | $896,000,000 |
| Underwriting discounts | $2.24 | $62,720,000 |
| Proceeds to Aurora (before expenses) | $29.76 | $654,720,000 |
| Proceeds to selling stockholders | $29.76 | $178,560,000 |

The underwriters have an option for 30 days to purchase up to 4,200,000 additional shares from us at the initial public offering price less underwriting discounts.

**Investing in our common stock involves a high degree of risk. See "Risk Factors" beginning on page 18.**

Neither the Securities and Exchange Commission nor any state securities commission has approved or disapproved of these securities or determined if this prospectus is truthful or complete.

**Joint Book-Running Managers**

Morgan Stanley &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Goldman Sachs &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; J.P. Morgan

**Co-Managers**

Barclays &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Citigroup &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Deutsche Bank Securities

The date of this prospectus is March 15, 2026.

---

## PROSPECTUS SUMMARY

### Overview

Aurora Quantum Computing, Inc. is a leading developer of quantum computing hardware and cloud-based quantum computing services. We design and manufacture superconducting quantum processors and provide access to our quantum computing systems through Aurora Quantum Cloud (AQC), a cloud platform that enables researchers, enterprises, and government agencies to run quantum algorithms without owning quantum hardware.

Founded in 2018 by Dr. James Liu and Dr. Priya Sharma at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, Aurora has developed proprietary quantum processor technology that has achieved industry-leading qubit counts and coherence times. Our latest generation processor, Aurora-Q1000, features 1,024 physical qubits with an average coherence time of 320 microseconds, enabling the execution of practical quantum algorithms for optimization, simulation, and machine learning applications.

### Our Market Opportunity

The global quantum computing market was valued at approximately $1.3 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach $12.8 billion by 2030, representing a compound annual growth rate of 58%, according to McKinsey & Company. Key market drivers include:

- Growing demand for computational solutions beyond classical computing capabilities in drug discovery, materials science, financial modeling, and logistics optimization
- Increasing government investment in quantum computing research and infrastructure, with over $35 billion committed globally through national quantum initiatives
- Enterprise adoption accelerating as quantum-classical hybrid algorithms demonstrate practical advantages for specific use cases

### Financial Performance Summary

| | FY2025 | FY2024 | FY2023 |
|---|---|---|---|
| Revenue | $127.4M | $78.2M | $41.6M |
| Revenue Growth | 62.9% | 88.0% | 124.3% |
| Gross Margin | 58.3% | 52.1% | 44.8% |
| Operating Loss | $(198.5M) | $(167.3M) | $(142.8M) |
| Net Loss | $(204.2M) | $(172.8M) | $(148.5M) |
| Cash and Equivalents | $312.6M | $198.4M | $87.2M |

## COMPANY HISTORY

### Timeline of Key Milestones

#### 2018 — Foundation
- **January 2018**: Aurora Quantum Computing incorporated in Delaware by co-founders Dr. James Liu (CEO) and Dr. Priya Sharma (CTO), based on quantum computing research conducted at MIT's Lincoln Laboratory
- **June 2018**: Secured $8 million seed round led by Andreessen Horowitz, with participation from Khosla Ventures and MIT's The Engine fund
- **September 2018**: Established first laboratory facility in Cambridge, Massachusetts; hired initial team of 12 quantum physicists and engineers

#### 2019 — First Processor
- **March 2019**: Demonstrated first superconducting quantum processor prototype with 32 qubits, achieving coherence times of 85 microseconds — 2.5x the then-industry average
- **August 2019**: Raised $45 million Series A round led by Sequoia Capital, with participation from Google Ventures, In-Q-Tel (the CIA's venture arm), and existing investors
- **November 2019**: Published peer-reviewed research in Nature Physics demonstrating quantum error correction advances that reduced logical error rates by 60%

#### 2020 — Cloud Platform Launch
- **February 2020**: Launched Aurora Quantum Cloud beta program with 15 academic and research institution partners, including Stanford, Caltech, and Oxford University
- **July 2020**: Raised $120 million Series B round co-led by Tiger Global and Fidelity Investments at a $780 million valuation
- **October 2020**: Achieved 64-qubit processor milestone (Aurora-Q64) with 150-microsecond coherence times; signed first commercial contracts with two pharmaceutical companies for drug discovery applications

#### 2021 — Commercial Traction
- **March 2021**: Opened 25,000 sq. ft. quantum processor fabrication facility in Somerville, Massachusetts, with capacity to produce 50 processors per year
- **June 2021**: Launched Aurora Quantum Cloud commercially; acquired first 28 paying enterprise customers across pharmaceutical, financial services, and materials science sectors
- **September 2021**: Raised $250 million Series C round led by SoftBank Vision Fund 2 at a $2.1 billion valuation; total funding reached $423 million
- **December 2021**: Headcount grew to 340 employees across Cambridge, MA and a new office in Boulder, Colorado

#### 2022 — Scaling Operations
- **February 2022**: Unveiled Aurora-Q256 processor with 256 qubits and 210-microsecond coherence times; demonstrated quantum advantage for specific molecular simulation tasks
- **May 2022**: Signed $35 million multi-year contract with the U.S. Department of Energy for quantum simulation of nuclear fusion plasma dynamics
- **August 2022**: Expanded into Europe with opening of London research center and partnership with Fraunhofer Institute in Germany
- **November 2022**: Revenue surpassed $10 million quarterly run rate for the first time; customer base grew to 85 enterprises

#### 2023 — Rapid Growth
- **January 2023**: Raised $180 million Series D round led by BlackRock at a $3.4 billion valuation
- **April 2023**: Launched financial services vertical with specialized quantum algorithms for portfolio optimization; signed Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Citadel as anchor clients
- **July 2023**: Achieved 512-qubit milestone (Aurora-Q512) with 270-microsecond coherence; partnered with BMW for supply chain optimization applications
- **October 2023**: Revenue reached $41.6 million for FY2023; customer base exceeded 150 enterprises

#### 2024 — Pre-IPO Acceleration
- **January 2024**: Raised $300 million Series E round led by T. Rowe Price and Wellington Management at a $5.2 billion pre-money valuation
- **March 2024**: Opened quantum processor fabrication center in Austin, Texas, tripling manufacturing capacity to 150 processors per year
- **June 2024**: Signed strategic partnership with Amazon Web Services for integration of Aurora quantum processors into AWS Braket platform
- **September 2024**: Unveiled Aurora-Q1000 processor with 1,024 qubits; demonstrated practical quantum advantage for portfolio risk modeling with JP Morgan
- **December 2024**: Revenue reached $78.2 million for FY2024; 230 enterprise customers across 12 countries; headcount of 780 employees

#### 2025 — IPO Year
- **March 2025**: Selected Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs, and J.P. Morgan as joint book-running managers for planned IPO
- **June 2025**: Signed $52 million multi-year contract with Pfizer for quantum-accelerated drug discovery
- **September 2025**: Revenue run rate exceeded $150 million annualized; customer base surpassed 310 enterprises
- **December 2025**: FY2025 revenue of $127.4 million, up 62.9% year-over-year; 312 enterprise customers; 920 employees

---

## USE OF PROCEEDS

We estimate that we will receive net proceeds of approximately $640 million from this offering (or approximately $765 million if the underwriters exercise their over-allotment option in full), after deducting underwriting discounts and estimated offering expenses.

We intend to use the net proceeds from this offering as follows:

| Use Category | Allocated Amount | % of Proceeds | Timeline | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **R&D — Next-gen Processor** | $250,000,000 | 39.1% | 2026-2028 | Development of the Aurora-Q4000 processor targeting 4,096+ qubits with error-corrected logical qubits; expansion of quantum algorithm research team; investment in topological qubit research program |
| **Manufacturing Expansion** | $150,000,000 | 23.4% | 2026-2027 | Construction of a new 60,000 sq. ft. advanced quantum processor fabrication facility in Austin, TX; procurement of dilution refrigerator systems and cryogenic equipment; target capacity of 300+ processors per year |
| **Cloud Infrastructure** | $100,000,000 | 15.6% | 2026-2028 | Expansion of Aurora Quantum Cloud data centers in Virginia, Frankfurt, and Tokyo; deployment of 200+ quantum processing units; development of hybrid classical-quantum orchestration platform |
| **Sales & Market Expansion** | $80,000,000 | 12.5% | 2026-2027 | Establishment of sales offices in Frankfurt, Tokyo, Singapore, and Seoul; hiring of 120+ enterprise sales and solutions engineering staff; channel partner development program |
| **Working Capital & General** | $60,000,000 | 9.4% | Ongoing | General corporate purposes including working capital, potential strategic acquisitions, and other corporate activities as determined by management |

### Dual-Class Share Structure

We have adopted a dual-class common stock structure:
- **Class A Common Stock**: One vote per share. All shares sold in this offering will be Class A shares.
- **Class B Common Stock**: Ten votes per share. Held exclusively by the co-founders. Class B shares automatically convert to Class A shares upon transfer (with limited exceptions) or upon the earlier of (i) seven years after the IPO or (ii) the date on which the co-founders collectively hold fewer than 10% of outstanding shares.

As of the completion of this offering, Dr. James Liu and Dr. Priya Sharma will collectively control approximately 67.6% of the total voting power, enabling them to control the outcome of matters submitted to stockholder vote, including the election of directors, amendments to our certificate of incorporation, and approval of significant corporate transactions.

---

## RISK FACTORS

### Technology and Product Risks

**Quantum computing technology is at an early stage and may not achieve widespread commercial viability.** While we have demonstrated quantum advantage for specific applications, fault-tolerant universal quantum computing has not yet been achieved by any company. Our business model depends on continued advancement of quantum hardware and algorithms. If quantum computing technology fails to deliver meaningful practical advantages over classical computing within the timeframes expected by the market, demand for our products and services could be significantly lower than projected.

**Our quantum processors have limited operational lifetimes.** Superconducting qubits are extremely sensitive to environmental interference and require operating temperatures near absolute zero (approximately 15 millikelvin). Component degradation, including Josephson junction aging and dielectric loss, typically requires processor replacement every 18-24 months. The cost of replacement processors and ongoing cryogenic infrastructure maintenance represents a significant recurring expense.

### Market and Competition Risks

**We face intense competition from well-funded competitors.** The quantum computing industry includes competitors with substantially greater resources, including IBM (127+ qubit processors), Google (72+ qubit processors), Microsoft (topological approach), Amazon (ion trap approach through IonQ partnership), and several well-funded startups including Rigetti Computing, IonQ, and PsiQuantum. Several of these competitors have announced roadmaps to achieve fault-tolerant quantum computing within similar or earlier timeframes than our own.
